title: Policy Development for Mandatory qPCR Halal Screening in Exports
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Global halal   food     trade     is     expanding     rapidly  ,   with   Muslim   consumers     demanding     high     standards     of     integrity     and     transparency  .   However  ,   cases     of     meat     adulteration     and   non-halal   contamination     continue     to     undermine     consumer     trust     and     threaten     market     access  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   offers     a     rapid  ,   accurate  ,   and     cost-effective     solution     for     verifying     animal     species     and     detecting   non-halal   ingredients     before     products     leave     exporting     countries  .   Establishing     mandatory     qPCR   halal   screening     for     exports     would     strengthen     compliance     with     international   halal   standards  ,   protect     national     reputations  ,   and     enhance     competitiveness   in   the   global   market  . By   integrating     molecular     diagnostics     into     national     food     control     systems  ,   governments     can     ensure     that     exported     products     consistently     meet   halal   requirements  ,   reducing     trade     disputes     and     safeguarding     access     to     lucrative   halal   markets  .
